items.title: Java 9 Additions To Optional
items.summary: Java 9 is coming! One of the changes are new methods on Optional: stream() , or() , and ifPresentOrElse() , which considerably improve Optional's API.

items.title: Comment Your &*☠# Code!
items.summary: A heartfelt rant / thoughtful talk arguing for more comments in code

items.title: Java 9 Additions To Stream
items.summary: Java 9 is coming! One of the many changes are new Stream methods: takeWhile , dropWhile , and ofNullable . For more fun with streams!

items.title: How To Implement hashCode Correctly
items.summary: So you wrote a nice equals implementation? Great! But now you have to implement hashCode as well. Let’s see how to do it correctly.

items.title: How To Implement equals Correctly
items.summary: A fundamental aspect of any Java class is its definition of equality. It is determined by a class's equals method. Let's see how to implement it correctly.

items.title: JEEConf 2016
items.summary: My take on JEEConf 2016: showing the talks I liked the best, raving about the community, romanticizing Kiev, and giving some feedback. Summary: awesome!

items.title: JUnit 5
items.summary: In this talk, I introduce JUnit 5 from basic tests to more advanced features like nesting, parameterization, parallelization, and extensions. We also discuss its architecture and compatibility with JUnit 4.

items.title: CodeFX Leveled Up
items.summary: A lot of things are happening right now: I'm writing a book, I'll speak at conferences, you can hire me, and to top it off, I gave this blog a new look.

items.title: A Doomed Code Review
items.summary: Code reviews should be brief, short, and focused. This is the story of how I fucked up on all those accounts and we still made it work.

items.title: Seven Reasons Against Blogging
items.summary: Many people will tell you how great blogging is but there are downsides, too, and they are rarely discussed. So let me share some reasons against blogging.

items.title: The Java Module System Beyond The Basics
items.summary: In this talk, I go beyond the module system basics and present more advanced features for those who want to become their team's module system expert

items.title: Building Atom On Gentoo
items.summary: See how to build Atom on Gentoo straight from the sources.

items.title: Costs And Benefits Of Comments
items.summary: As with most things in software development the ultimate currency for comments is time. This is an analysis of the costs and benefits of comments.

items.title: Implied Readability With requires transitive
items.summary: In Java 9 a module must read another to use its API. With implied readability a 3rd module passes the dependency on, allowing the 1st to read the 2nd.

items.title: Beware Of findFirst() And findAny()
items.summary: Stream.findFirst() and findAny() work with any number of elements in the stream. Make sure to reduce(toOnlyElement()) if there should be at most one.

items.title: Hello 2016!
items.summary: In 2016 I want to continue blogging, branch out into new areas, work on my private projects, learn me a Haskell, and prevent exhaustion.Wow, so much to do.

items.title: Goodbye 2015!
items.summary: 2015 is over and I'm looking back.How did it go, which things worked out and which didn't. And how come I'm feeling so tired recently.

items.title: Jigsaw Hands-On Guide
items.summary: A Jigsaw tutorial explaining how to create modules, state dependencies between them, and use the module system as a service locator to decouple modules.

items.title: JDeps Maven Plugin 0.2 Released
items.summary: With v0.2 the JDeps Maven Plugin allows the creation of flexible exceptions from build-breaking for a self-paced preparation for Java 9 and Project Jigsaw.

items.title: Six-Month Delay Of Java 9 Release
items.summary: Mark Reinhold proposed a six-month delay of JSR 376 / Project Jigsaw and thus of the Java 9 release. According to this JDK 9 would come out in March 2017.

items.title: JUnit Lambda - The Prototype
items.summary: JUnit Lambda will eventually bring us JUnit 5. This is a discussion of the recent prototype, its features, core principles and compatibility considerations.

items.title: JavaOne 2015: Under The Hood Of Project Jigsaw
items.summary: JavaOne 2015 saw a series of talks by the Project Jigsaw team about modularity in Java 9. This one gives a peek under the hood discussing layers and class loaders.

items.title: JavaOne 2015: Advanced Modular Development
items.summary: JavaOne 2015 saw a series of talks by the Project Jigsaw team about modularity in Java 9. This one details different migration scenarios.

items.title: JavaOne 2015: Introduction to Modular Development
items.summary: JavaOne 2015 saw a series of talks by the Project Jigsaw team about modularity in Java 9. This one introduces the basic concepts.

items.title: JavaOne 2015: Prepare For JDK 9
items.summary: JavaOne 2015 saw a series of talks by the Project Jigsaw team about modularity in Java 9. This one explains how to prepare for it.

items.title: Will There Be Module Hell?
items.summary: Java 9's Project Jigsaw promises to solve JAR hell but falls short when it comes to conflicting versions. Will there be module hell instead?

items.title: JAR Hell
items.summary: A list of the problems that make up JAR hell (classpath hell, dependency hell) and how they relate to development tools like Maven and OSGi.

items.title: Apache JMeter Tutorial
items.summary: Want to load test your web app? Then you should know about Apache JMeter! This tutorial explains the basics and how to create & execute a test plan.

items.title: A Taxonomy Of Comments
items.summary: A taxonomy of source code comments that enables further discussion about clean code and comments.

items.title: Stream Performance - Your Ideas
items.summary: Another post about stream performance - this one implements your ideas about how else to approach the topic.

items.title: Stream Performance
items.summary: A close look at stream performance. How do they compare to for and for-each loops oder arrays and lists. And what role plays boxing?

items.title: Java 8 SE Optional, a strict approach
items.summary: Stephen Colebourne presented his pragmatic approach to using Optional. I argue for a stricter one that gets us further without considerable downsides.

items.title: Interview About Comments On DZone
items.summary: Matt Werner from DZone interviewed me about my stance on comments.

items.title: Impulse: "Adventures On The Road to Valhalla"
items.summary: A summary of Brian Goetz' talk "Adventures On The Road to Valhalla" given at JVMLS in August 2015. Focused on generic specialization and the two prototypes.

items.title: All About Project Jigsaw On InfoQ
items.summary: My posts about Project Jigsaw got polished and published on InfoQ.

items.title: Thoughts On Comments
items.summary: My rant to comment your fucking code sparked some interesting conversations. Here we discuss some of your and my thoughts on the topic of comments.

items.title: Comment Your Fucking Code!
items.summary: You think your code is so clean that it doesn't need comments? Then this rant is just for you!

items.title: Casting In Java 8 (And Beyond?)
items.summary: Proposal to implement new casting methods on Java's Class . They aim to fulfill the need for improved ways to cast which was created by Java 8.

items.title: The Features Project Jigsaw Brings To Java 9
items.summary: A detailed presentation of the features Project Jigsaw brings to Java 9: modularization, encapsulation, configuration, performance, and more.

items.title: Motivation And Goals Of Project Jigsaw
items.summary: A look at how Project Jigsaw (coming in Java 9) aims to solve JAR/classpath hell and at its goals to improve security, maintainability and performance.

items.title: LibFX 0.3.0 Released
items.summary: Release post for LibFX 0.3.0 including pointers to GitHub, feature descriptions, Maven coordinates and the Javadoc.

items.title: Transforming Collections
items.summary: Transforming collections are a view onto another collection, making it appear to be of a different parametric type. They are available in LibFX 0.3.0.

items.title: JavaFX, Project Jigsaw and JEP 253
items.summary: JEP253 aims to prepare JavaFX for Project Jigsaw by defining public APIs for functionality that will become inaccessible due to modularization.

items.title: First Release of JDeps Maven Plugin
items.summary: The JDeps Maven Plugin will break a project's build if it contains dependencies on JDK-internal APIs. This helps to prepare for Java 9.

items.title: Impulse: "Crafted Design"
items.summary: Summary of the architecture described by Sandro Mancuso in his talk "Crafted Design", held at GeeCON2014.

items.title: LibFX 0.2.1 Released
items.summary: Release post for LibFX 0.2.1 including pointers to GitHub, feature descriptions, Maven coordinates and the Javadoc.

items.title: How Java 9 And Project Jigsaw May Break Your Code
items.summary: With Java 9 comes Project Jigsaw - a modularization of the JDK - which will break existing code. An overview over the planned changes lets you see whether yours is affected.

items.title: Getting Rid Of Anonymous Classes
items.summary: Anonymous classes are verbose and obfuscating. Functional implementations can oust them from their last strongholds (mainly abstract classes).

items.title: Interface Evolution With Default Methods – Part II: Interfaces
items.summary: Why interface evolution with default methods does not work for whole interfaces - at least not smooth enough to be practical.

items.title: Impulse: "Agile Architecture"
items.summary: A summary of the talk "Agile Architecture" given by Molly Dishman and Martin Fowler as the keynote at the O'Reilly Software Architecture Conference.

items.title: Roll Your Own Pirate-Elvis Operator
items.summary: Java has no Elvis operator (or null coalescing operator / null-safe member selection) but with lambda expressions / method references you can roll your own.

items.title: Test Collection Implementations with Guava
items.summary: Here's how to use Guava-Testlib to easily and thoroughly test your own Java collection implementations.

items.title: Interface Evolution With Default Methods - Part I: Methods
items.summary: Patterns for interface evolution with default methods: gradually add, replace and remove interface methods without breaking client code.

items.title: Value-Based Classes
items.summary: An explanation of value-based classes in Java 8. Why do they exist? What are their limitations? How (not) to use them?

items.title: Running Android Emulator With HAXM On Thinkpad T440p
items.summary: Quick guide to how to use the Android emulator with HAXM (based on VT-x) on a Thinkpad T440p.

items.title: Everything You Need To Know About Default Methods
items.summary: Covering literally everything there is to know about Java 8's default methods.

items.title: New Javadoc Tags @apiNote, @implSpec, and @implNote
items.summary: There are new Javadoc tags used in Java 8: @apiNote , @implSpec , and @implNote . Take a look at their history, meaning and use on command line and with Maven.

items.title: Hello 2015!
items.summary: I'm laying out my new year's resolutions for 2015.

items.title: Goodbye 2014!
items.summary: I'm taking a look at my open source contributions and blogging achievements in 2014.

items.title: Instances of Non-Capturing Lambdas
items.summary: See how Java's creation of instances of non-capturing lambda expressions can lead to unexpected and possibly bug-inducing behavior.
